guardrail_evaluations.explanation: This deal carries no list price or proposed price — it is a rev-share partnership with no subscription discount. The discount_pct guardrail is technically inapplicable, but the deal structure bypasses standard pricing entirely, which warrants the same RevOps scrutiny this rule was designed to enforce.

No monetary discount is being granted, so the numeric threshold is not breached. However, the AE has already routed this to RevOps per policy, which is the correct path — this rule's intent is satisfied even though the mechanism is non-standard.

Because there is no baseline subscription fee, Clay's margin on the initial deal is 0% until rev-share remittances begin arriving. The estimated $400K–$1.2M annual rev-share contribution has no corresponding COGS baseline to benchmark against standard margin rules, making a conventional margin calculation meaningless — the guardrail fires on 0% actual.

With list_price and proposed_price both at $0, the computed margin is 0% — below the 25% absolute floor. This is a structural artifact of a rev-share deal, not a true margin violation, but the guardrail formally fires and requires CFO/leadership sign-off before the deal can proceed. The deal must be re-framed with an expected-margin treatment of the rev-share economics before it clears this gate.

alternative_structures.label: Hybrid: Minimum Subscription Base + Reduced Rev Share
alternative_structures.description: Introduce a $60K/yr baseline subscription fee for white-label rights (covering Clay's infrastructure/support COGS floor), and reduce the rev-share rate from 25% to 18%. Reverberate gets a lower rev-share drag as volumes scale; Clay gets predictable, margin-positive base revenue that satisfies the absolute margin floor guardrail.
alternative_structures.rationale: A $60K floor priced at approximately 40% margin (using the standard 40% list-margin assumption) transforms the deal from a pure contingency play into a hybrid that passes both margin guardrails. The rev-share upside is preserved while Clay is not entirely dependent on Reverberate's billing execution. Reverberate benefits from a lower rev-share rate (18% vs 25%), improving their unit economics per client as ACV scales.

alternative_structures.label: Full Rev Share with Escrow Reserve and Audit Rights
alternative_structures.description: Keep the 25% rev-share structure but require: (a) Reverberate holds a $75K rolling escrow for rev-share obligations, replenished quarterly; (b) Clay has quarterly audit rights over Reverberate Engine subscription billings; (c) the agreement includes a minimum annual guarantee of $200K in rev-share remittance, or the white-label license reverts to standard licensing terms. No upfront subscription fee.
alternative_structures.rationale: The minimum annual guarantee of $200K de-risks Clay's margin exposure — the deal cannot close at 0% revenue. The escrow and audit rights reduce counterparty and reporting risk, which is the primary structural weakness of a pure rev-share. Reverberate retains the full 25% rev-share economics they requested. Margin estimate of ~35% is approximate, based on rev-share revenue having lower direct COGS than a seat-based subscription (no seat provisioning overhead), but remains uncertain pending CFO review.

alternative_structures.label: White-Label License Fee + Co-Marketing Value Exchange (No Rev Share)
alternative_structures.description: Replace the rev-share entirely with a flat annual white-label license fee of $120K/yr on a 24-month term. In exchange, Clay provides expanded co-marketing commitments: joint GTM content, featured partner placement, and joint pipeline referrals from Reverberate's 8 top-50 client relationships. Reverberate keeps all subscription revenue from Reverberate Engine clients.
alternative_structures.rationale: A flat license fee of $120K/yr is fully predictable, passes all margin guardrails at the standard 40% assumption, and removes Clay's dependency on Reverberate's rev-share remittance accuracy. Reverberate's upside is uncapped — they retain 100% of Reverberate Engine billings — which is compelling at their growth trajectory. The trade-off is Clay foregoes the $400K–$1.2M rev-share upside scenario, but captures a cleaner, auditable revenue line. Best fit if Reverberate's volumes prove hard to audit or if legal complexity of rev-share is a concern.

pricing.reasoning_summary: This deal is structurally non-standard: list_price and proposed_price are both $0, making all standard discount and margin guardrails formally non-applicable or technically firing at 0%. The gross margin absolute floor guardrail (block_absolute) fires on the 0% computed margin and must be resolved before the deal proceeds — the appropriate resolution is either a reframed structure (see alternatives) or explicit CFO override acknowledging rev-share economics. All margin estimates in this output use the working assumption that list price carries a 40% gross margin, per standing instruction; the CFO should substitute actual COGS figures for the white-label infrastructure cost before signing off. The most immediate risk is precedent: approving a pure rev-share at $0 baseline for a PLG customer could be cited by other agencies seeking similar terms, so deal governance and MFN language should be reviewed by legal.

performance_obligations.name: White-label license to Clay tables and waterfalls (Reverberate Engine)
performance_obligations.description: Grant of rights to Reverberate to package and resell Clay's tables/waterfalls functionality under the Reverberate Engine brand to its clients over the 24-month term.
performance_obligations.rationale: The license is distinct (capable of being used on its own and separately identifiable from co-marketing/early access). Because consideration is a 25% rev share tied to end-customer subscription usage, recognition follows the sales/usage-based royalty exception (ASC 606-10-55-65).

performance_obligations.name: Co-marketing rights with client-attributed case studies
performance_obligations.description: Mutual marketing rights including the ability to publish case studies referencing Reverberate's clients.
performance_obligations.rationale: Co-marketing is bundled with the partnership and unlikely separately identifiable from the white-label license; no incremental fee is exchanged. Treat as a marketing benefit, not a separate PO unless determined to be a material right.

performance_obligations.name: Early access to new Clay features (30-day advance)
performance_obligations.description: Reverberate receives access to new Clay features 30 days before public GA.
performance_obligations.rationale: Early access is an ongoing stand-ready obligation distinct from the white-label rights. Because no separately stated fee exists, allocation will rely on ESP; revenue recognized ratably over the 24-month term.

variable_consideration_flags.explanation: All consideration is contingent on Reverberate's downstream subscription sales. Under the royalty exception for licenses of IP, recognize as the underlying sales/usage occurs; no estimation/constraint required upfront. However, total contract value cannot be fixed and is expected to range $400K–$1.2M/yr.
Because the white-label license is bundled with early-access and co-marketing, allocation across POs requires ESP. Some allocation may shift consideration away from the royalty-eligible PO.
If Clay provides case studies/co-marketing as a service, this could be a separate PO; otherwise it's a marketing arrangement outside the scope of the customer contract. Judgment required.
Stand-ready obligation requires ESP estimation since no separate fee is charged. Allocation reduces the portion of consideration eligible for the royalty exception.

contract_modification_risk.explanation: The arrangement is a partnership without fixed consideration; any change in rev-share rate, scope of white-labeled features, or addition of new Clay products into the bundle would likely constitute a contract modification under ASC 606-10-25-10. Additionally, if Reverberate's clients overlap with existing Clay direct customers, principal-vs-agent and potential consideration-payable-to-customer questions (ASC 606-10-32-25) may re-open the analysis.

recognized_revenue_schedule.notes: Midpoint estimate of $400K–$1.2M rev-share range; recognized as Reverberate's end-customer subscriptions occur (royalty exception). Actual will vary with downstream sales.
Midpoint estimate; recognized as underlying sales occur. A portion may be re-allocated to the early-access PO and recognized ratably.

red_flags.label: No fixed transaction price — entirely rev-share
red_flags.explanation: Deal has $0 ACV/TCV in CRM; all economics depend on Reverberate's downstream sales. Requires CFO sign-off on revenue accounting model and disclosure treatment before signing.

red_flags.label: Principal vs. agent determination
red_flags.explanation: Need to determine whether Clay is principal or agent for end-customer subscriptions sold under the Reverberate Engine brand (ASC 606-10-55-36). Affects gross-vs-net presentation.

red_flags.label: Channel conflict / consideration payable to a customer
red_flags.explanation: Reverberate has 8 of Clay's top-50 customers as their clients. If those clients migrate to Reverberate Engine, the 25% rev share could be viewed as consideration payable to a customer (ASC 606-10-32-25), reducing direct-contract revenue.

red_flags.label: Early-access feature obligation
red_flags.explanation: 30-day advance access is an ongoing stand-ready obligation that must be separately allocated using ESP and recognized ratably, complicating the otherwise royalty-based model.

red_flags.label: Co-marketing / case study rights
red_flags.explanation: Confirm whether case-study delivery constitutes a distinct service obligation or falls outside the contract scope.

red_flags.label: Approval matrix gap
red_flags.explanation: Standard pricing approval matrix does not contemplate partnership/rev-share deals; partnership-deal governance and legal review required.

asc606.reasoning_summary: This is a partnership contract with no fixed consideration; revenue is a 25% rev share on Reverberate's downstream subscription sales, which qualifies for the sales/usage-based royalty exception under ASC 606-10-55-65. However, the bundle includes co-marketing and 30-day early-access rights that may be distinct POs requiring ESP allocation, and there are material principal-vs-agent and consideration-payable-to-a-customer questions given client overlap. Multiple interacting variable-consideration sources and modification risks drive low confidence. This is an automated estimate; final determination requires CFO/auditor review.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate proposes to white-label Clay tables and waterfalls under their own branded product 'Reverberate Engine,' sold directly to their end clients with no visible Clay attribution.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A full white-label grant gives Reverberate sublicensing rights to Clay's core product surface with zero brand association. This erodes Clay's direct distribution in mid-market RevOps, which is Clay's primary PLG growth segment. If Reverberate's clients never know they're using Clay, Clay cannot convert those end-users to direct subscribers at term-end or at churn. Worse, a white-label clause without scope limits can be construed as a perpetual, transferable IP license — which is a block-level risk if the company is ever acquired.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: White-label rights are granted solely to Reverberate Growth, Inc. for use within the 'Reverberate Engine' service offering, and are (a) non-transferable, (b) non-sublicensable beyond Reverberate's direct end clients, (c) limited to Clay's tables and waterfall workflow surfaces as of the Effective Date and any surfaces explicitly added by written amendment, and (d) subject to Clay's right to display a 'Powered by Clay' attribution mark in a location and format mutually agreed in writing within 30 days of signing. White-label rights terminate automatically and immediately upon expiration or termination of this Agreement.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: If Reverberate rejects 'Powered by Clay' attribution, accept removal of visible in-product attribution in exchange for: (1) Clay named as the infrastructure provider in Reverberate Engine's public documentation and pitch decks, and (2) a right-of-first-refusal for Reverberate's end clients to convert to direct Clay subscriptions at term end at a pre-agreed rate.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has not previously executed a white-label partnership of this scope; referral and co-sell agreements exist but do not include product reskinning rights.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate proposes a 25% revenue share on all Reverberate Engine subscriptions paid by their clients, remitted quarterly at net-45 from end-of-quarter close, with no baseline subscription fee from Reverberate to Clay.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A pure rev-share with no floor means Clay's revenue is entirely contingent on Reverberate's commercial success, billing accuracy, and good-faith reporting — with no audit right or minimum guarantee. At $400K–$1.2M estimated annual contribution, a 12-month gap between billing and audit discovery could represent $100K–$300K in undetected underpayment. Additionally, 25% with no ACV floor means Clay earns the same rate on a $500/mo client as a $50K/mo client, which under-monetizes the high-end and cannot be corrected mid-term without a renegotiation.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Revenue share is set at 25% of Reverberate Engine gross subscription revenue attributable to Clay-powered features, subject to: (a) a minimum annual guarantee of $150,000 USD payable in equal quarterly installments regardless of actual rev-share accrual ('Minimum Guarantee'); (b) Clay's right, no more than once per calendar year, to audit Reverberate's relevant billing records upon 15 days written notice, at Clay's expense unless an underpayment of >5% is discovered, in which case audit costs are borne by Reverberate; (c) late payment interest of 1.5% per month on any amount not remitted within 15 days of the net-45 due date; and (d) Clay's right to renegotiate the rev-share rate if Reverberate Engine ACV exceeds $3M in any trailing 12-month period.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: If Reverberate rejects the Minimum Guarantee, accept a tiered rev-share with no floor: 20% on Reverberate Engine ACV up to $1M/yr, 27% on ACV $1M–$3M/yr, 30% above $3M/yr — retaining audit rights and late-payment interest as non-negotiable.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has structured tiered rev-share in at least one integration partnership context, but no white-label-specific precedent with a minimum guarantee is on file.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate requests co-marketing rights including the ability to publish case studies featuring their end clients, with attribution to Reverberate as the author of the engagement outcome (not Clay).
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Co-marketing rights that attribute all client outcomes to Reverberate without mention of Clay actively suppress Clay's brand awareness in the segment these clients represent. Given that Reverberate's founder has already published content arguing for 'vendor-agnostic' agency partnerships, there is a real risk that public case studies actively obscure Clay's role, training Reverberate's audience to see the agency — not the underlying tool — as the source of value. This weakens Clay's direct sales motion into mid-market RevOps over the 24-month term.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Co-marketing rights are granted on a mutual, non-exclusive basis. Case studies and joint content may be published by either party subject to: (a) mutual written approval of each piece of content no less than 5 business days before publication; (b) any case study referencing Clay-powered workflows must include a 'Powered by Clay' notation in the body of the content; (c) neither party shall publish content that disparages or positions the other as a commodity input; and (d) Clay reserves the right to reference this partnership and Reverberate's client outcomes (in aggregate, without naming end clients) in Clay's own marketing materials with Reverberate's prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: If Reverberate rejects mandatory 'Powered by Clay' notation in case study body copy, accept footnote-level attribution ('Built on Clay infrastructure') in exchange for Clay's right to list Reverberate as a named partner on Clay's website and in investor materials.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has required 'Powered by Clay' attribution in at least one OEM-adjacent integration agreement; agency-specific co-marketing precedent is limited.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate requests early access to new Clay features 30 days before public launch.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Granting a 30-day early access window to a white-label partner means Reverberate can build, train clients on, and market Clay features before Clay's own GTM team has launched them publicly. If Reverberate's clients are in Clay's direct ICP, Reverberate could pre-empt Clay's own PLG launch narrative and lock clients into Reverberate Engine workflows before Clay's direct sales team can engage. It also creates an implied obligation that could conflict with Clay's product roadmap security and NDA posture with enterprise clients.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide Reverberate with early access to new platform features designated by Clay as 'Partner Preview' features no less than 14 calendar days prior to general availability. Early access is (a) limited to Reverberate's internal team for testing and workflow development only — not client-facing deployment — until general availability; (b) subject to a mutual NDA covering all pre-GA features; (c) expressly excluded for features under regulatory review, features gated to specific customer tiers, and features Clay designates as confidential for competitive reasons; and (d) not a guarantee of feature parity or roadmap commitment.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: If Reverberate insists on 30-day client-facing early access, limit it to features Clay has already announced publicly (e.g., at a conference or on the blog) but not yet shipped — ensuring no competitive intelligence leakage — while retaining the NDA and carve-outs above.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has offered 14-day beta access to select integration partners under NDA; 30-day client-facing early access has not previously been granted.

redline.one_line_summary: We can structure this partnership, but we must (1) scope and limit the white-label grant with 'Powered by Clay' attribution, (2) add a $150K annual minimum guarantee plus audit rights to the rev-share, (3) restrict early access to internal-only pre-GA use under NDA, and (4) require mutual approval and Clay attribution on all co-marketing before this can ship.
redline.reasoning_summary: This deal is a novel partnership archetype for Clay — no existing white-label precedent applies cleanly, and the combination of a full white-label grant with zero floor revenue and client-facing early access creates compounding brand suppression and revenue exposure in Clay's core PLG segment. The white-label clause as proposed is the primary block: without scope limits, non-transferability, and attribution, it functions as a perpetual, sublicensable IP license that survives any Reverberate acquisition. The rev-share structure's lack of a minimum guarantee and audit rights is a secondary block given the deal's estimated $400K–$1.2M annual contribution range. Counters proposed are novel but grounded in analogous OEM and integration partnership structures Clay has used; legal sign-off is required before any counter is sent.

required_approvers.rationale: This is a partnership deal (deal_type = 'partnership'), which is explicitly flagged as falling outside the standard pricing matrix and requiring CEO + Strategic Partnership Review approval per the default rule.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: White-label, rev-share, co-marketing, and early-access clauses require Legal review
required_approvers.rationale: The deal contains multiple non-standard clauses (white_label_branding, rev_share_25pct, co_marketing_with_client_attribution, early_access_30d_advance) flagged by the Redline Agent as 'block'-level risk. Legal must scope the white-label grant, add minimum guarantees and audit rights to rev-share, restrict early access to NDA-protected internal use, and ensure Clay attribution in co-marketing before the deal can proceed.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Rev-share economics and zero-margin structure require CFO sign-off
required_approvers.rationale: The Pricing Agent flagged a 'block_absolute' guardrail violation: gross margin is 0% due to zero list price and zero proposed price. The ASC 606 Agent flagged 'block_without_approval' on the variable-consideration treatment. The deal must either be reframed with a minimum subscription base or explicitly approved by CFO with acknowledgment of rev-share accounting and audit risk.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Non-standard partnership structure with contingent economics requires RevOps governance
required_approvers.rationale: The deal bypasses standard pricing guardrails and is flagged by Pricing as non-standard revenue structure. RevOps must review partnership governance, audit rights, and reporting cadence to ensure compliance with Clay's vendor-management and financial-reporting standards.

blockers_to_address_first: CFO must sign off on the zero-margin, 100% rev-share structure (block_absolute guardrail) — either approve the reframed alternative structures (e.g., $60K baseline + 18% rev-share or $150K minimum guarantee + audit rights) or provide explicit executive override acknowledging rev-share accounting complexity and audit risk.

Legal must resolve the white-label clause scope: non-transferability, non-sublicensability, attribution requirement ('Powered by Clay'), and termination-on-exit language must be in place before any counter is sent to Reverberate.

Legal must add minimum annual guarantee ($150K suggested) and audit rights (no more than once per calendar year, 15-day notice, 5% underpayment threshold for cost reallocation) to the rev-share structure.
Legal must restrict early-access obligation to Reverberate's internal team under NDA, excluding client-facing deployment until general availability.
Legal must require mutual written approval and 'Powered by Clay' attribution (body or footnote) for all case studies and co-marketing materials.
ASC 606 and CFO must jointly determine principal-vs-agent classification and confirm revenue-recognition treatment (royalty exception vs. allocation/ratable) before signing.

CFO/RevOps must establish audit and reporting cadence to monitor Reverberate's downstream billing and rev-share remittance; Q-by-Q reconciliation and annual true-up language must be in the final agreement.

Pricing/Finance must re-model the deal under one of the three Pricing Agent alternatives (hybrid minimum + reduced rev-share, escrow + minimum guarantee, or flat license fee) to satisfy guardrails or obtain CFO exception.
approval.one_line_summary: This partnership routes to AE Manager → CFO + Legal + RevOps (parallel) → CEO. Expect 10–12 business days. Must resolve white-label scope + attribution, add $150K minimum guarantee + audit rights to rev-share, restrict early access to NDA-protected internal use, and get CFO sign-off on zero-margin structure before Legal sends counters.
approval.reasoning_summary: This is a novel partnership archetype for Clay with no clean standard-matrix mapping. The deal_type = 'partnership' triggers the default CEO rule, and the combination of block-level Redline findings (white-label scope, rev-share floor, early-access risk, co-marketing attribution), a block_absolute margin guardrail, and block_without_approval ASC 606 variable-consideration flag means every gating function (Legal, CFO, RevOps) must sign off before CEO can approve. The approval chain is unusually parallel at step 2 because all three functions have independent veto rights and none can meaningfully proceed until the others clear their blockers — Legal's redline counters inform CFO's margin analysis, CFO's guardrail decision informs RevOps' governance model, and RevOps' audit controls inform Legal's contract terms. Confidence is low because the deal lies outside Clay's precedent set and requires multiple cross-functional consensus gates.

text.text: Deal Review: Reverberate Engine White-Label Partnership

*Customer:* Reverberate Growth (`reverberategrowth.com`) · PLG/Self-Serve · GTM Agency · #18 Top-50 Agency
*ACV:* $0 (pure rev-share) · *Est. Annual Rev-Share Contribution:* $400K–$1.2M · *Term:* 24 months
*Effective Discount:* N/A · *Margin (current structure):* 0% — block_absolute guardrail triggered
*Approval Chain:* AE Manager → CFO + Legal + RevOps (parallel, all veto-eligible) → CEO · *Est. Cycle Time:* 10–12 business days

*What the deal team needs to know:*

• *White-label grant is the primary block.* As proposed, the clause is effectively a perpetual, transferable IP license with zero Clay attribution — Legal must add non-transferability, non-sublicensability, scope limits to current surfaces, and 'Powered by Clay' attribution before any counter goes to Reverberate. No counter should be sent until Legal clears this.

• *Zero-margin structure triggers a CFO block before this can proceed.* The gross margin absolute floor guardrail fires at 0% (deal has $0 list price, $0 proposed price). CFO must either approve one of the three Pricing Agent alternatives — hybrid $60K baseline + 18% rev-share, $150K minimum guarantee + escrow + audit rights, or flat $120K/yr license — or provide an explicit executive override with an acknowledged rev-share accounting treatment. ASC 606 principal-vs-agent classification also requires joint CFO/auditor determination.

• *Precedent risk is material and should be flagged to CEO proactively.* Approving a pure rev-share at $0 baseline for a PLG customer with white-label + early-access + co-marketing rights creates a template other agencies can cite. Legal and RevOps should confirm MFN posture and partnership governance guardrails are in place before CEO review so this deal doesn't set an unconstrained precedent.

elements.text: *AE Owner:* Devon Wright · *AE Manager:* Sarah Goldstein · *Close Target:* 2026-06-01 · review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

slack_post.plaintext_fallback:
DEAL REVIEW: Reverberate Engine White-Label Partnership (Reverberate Growth)
Structure: 0 ACV / pure 25% rev-share; est. $400K–$1.2M/yr contribution; 24-month term. No baseline fee.
Approval chain: AE Manager → CFO + Legal + RevOps (parallel) → CEO. Est. 10–12 business days.
Top blockers: (1) White-label clause is a block — Legal must scope grant + add Clay attribution before any counter is sent. (2) 0% margin triggers CFO block_absolute — must reframe structure or obtain executive override. (3) Precedent risk is high — CEO and Legal should align on MFN/governance guardrails before approval.
AE Owner: Devon Wright | Review filed by Kiln · agent-driven.

ae_email_draft.subject: [Reverberate] Deal-desk review complete — 3 blockers before this can move
ae_email_draft.body_markdown:
Devon, the deal-desk review is done and this deal is **blocked from submission** until three things are resolved — none of which you can close yourself, but you need to drive them.

1. **Get CFO on the calendar this week.** The zero-margin structure triggers a `block_absolute` guardrail. CFO must either approve one of the reframed alternatives (recommended: $60K baseline + 18% rev-share, or $150K annual minimum guarantee + audit rights) or provide an explicit executive override before Legal can draft counters.
2. **Loop in Legal now — do not send any counter to Reverberate yet.** The white-label clause as proposed is a perpetual, potentially transferable IP license. Legal must scope it (non-transferable, non-sublicensable, 'Powered by Clay' attribution, auto-terminates on exit) and add audit rights + minimum guarantee language to the rev-share before anything goes to Reverberate.
3. **Flag the channel-conflict risk to your manager Sarah before step 2 approvals kick off.** Eight of Reverberate's clients are in Clay's top-50 direct customer base; CFO and Legal both need to know this upfront for the principal-vs-agent ASC 606 determination.

**Approval chain you're walking into:** Sarah (AE Manager, Step 1) → CFO + Legal + RevOps in parallel (Step 2, all three have independent veto rights) → CEO (Step 3). Budget 10–12 business days once blockers are cleared — the June 1 close date is at risk if CFO isn't engaged by end of this week.

customer_email_draft.subject: Reverberate Engine Partnership — Clay's Position on Structure and Key Terms
customer_email_draft.body_markdown:
We appreciate the Reverberate team bringing this forward formally, and we understand the ambition behind the Reverberate Engine proposition — productizing what you've built on Clay for your client base is a natural next step, and the strategic alignment here is real. That said, because this structure sits entirely outside our standard pricing and approval matrix, we need to be transparent that several of the proposed terms require material changes before we can move toward a signed agreement.

On the **white-label grant**: we can support white-labeling Clay tables and waterfalls within the Reverberate Engine offering, but the license as proposed needs to be explicitly scoped — non-transferable, non-sublicensable beyond your direct end clients, limited to the surfaces in scope at signing, and subject to a 'Powered by Clay' attribution mark in a mutually agreed format. We're open to discussing where and how that attribution surfaces, but some form of it is a requirement.

On the **rev-share structure**: 25% of Reverberate Engine gross subscription revenue is a workable rate, but a pure rev-share with no floor and no audit rights isn't something we can approve at the estimated $400K–$1.2M annual contribution range. We need either a minimum annual guarantee of $150K with quarterly installments and Clay audit rights no more than once per year, or we move to a hybrid structure with a modest baseline subscription fee and a reduced rev-share rate — our teams are prepared to model both with you.

On **early access**: we can offer 14-day advance access to features designated for partner preview, but it must be restricted to Reverberate's internal team under mutual NDA until general availability — not client-facing deployment — and excludes features under regulatory review or competitive confidentiality holds.

On **co-marketing**: we support joint content and case studies with mutual written approval on each piece, and any content referencing Clay-powered workflows must include Clay attribution in body copy or footnote — the specific format is negotiable.

This deal requires CEO, CFO, and Legal sign-off on our side, and we expect that process to take 10–12 business days once we align on the structural parameters above. To keep your June 1 target in view, we'd like to schedule a working session with your founder and our deal team this week to close the gap on the rev-share floor and attribution questions before we route internally — can you confirm availability?

sections.heading: Headline
sections.content_markdown: Reverberate Growth (top-18 GTM agency, $12M ARR, 80 employees) proposes a 24-month white-label partnership: Clay's tables and waterfalls are resold under their 'Reverberate Engine' brand, with Clay earning a 25% revenue share (est. $400K–$1.2M/yr) and no baseline subscription fee. AE Devon Wright has no authority over partnership deals; routed to deal-desk by RevOps.

sections.heading: Pricing Summary
sections.content_markdown:
- **List / proposed price:** $0 (no subscription fee)
- **Effective discount:** N/A — pure rev-share structure
- **Estimated annual rev-share contribution:** $400K–$1.2M (midpoint ~$800K)
- **Margin:** Formally 0% at signing; triggers **block_absolute** guardrail (floor: 25%)
- **Recommended alternative structures (pick one before closing):**
  1. **Hybrid:** $60K/yr baseline + 18% rev-share → ~40% margin, clears all guardrails
  2. **Escrow + Minimum Guarantee:** Keep 25% rev-share; add $75K rolling escrow, $200K annual guarantee, annual audit rights → ~35% margin
  3. **Flat License Fee:** $120K/yr, no rev-share → 40% margin, cleanest audit trail, forfeits upside

sections.heading: Risk Findings
sections.content_markdown:
**Redline — BLOCK**
- **White-label grant (high):** As proposed, this is effectively a perpetual, transferable IP sublicense with no Clay attribution and no scope limit. Survives acquisition of Reverberate. Counter required: non-transferable, non-sublicensable, scoped to current surfaces, + *'Powered by Clay'* attribution mark.
- **Rev-share structure (high):** No floor, no audit rights, no minimum guarantee. Up to $300K/yr underpayment exposure. Counter required: $150K annual minimum guarantee + annual audit right (5% underpayment threshold triggers cost shift).
- **Early access — 30 days (medium):** Client-facing pre-GA deployment lets Reverberate pre-empt Clay's own PLG launch narrative. Counter required: restrict to Reverberate internal/testing only under mutual NDA; cap at 14 days or limit to publicly announced-but-unshipped features.
- **Co-marketing attribution (medium):** All case study credit flows to Reverberate; Clay brand suppressed in its own PLG segment. Counter required: mutual approval gate, 'Powered by Clay' in body or footnote of all joint content.

**ASC 606 — BLOCK (requires CFO sign-off before signing)**
- 100% variable consideration (royalty exception applies per ASC 606-10-55-65); CRM shows $0 ACV/TCV — revenue recognition model must be documented and approved.
- **Principal vs. agent** determination needed: affects gross-vs-net presentation. 8 Reverberate clients overlap with Clay's top-50 direct customers — 25% rev-share on those accounts may constitute *consideration payable to a customer* (ASC 606-10-32-25), reducing direct-contract revenue.
- Early-access PO requires separate ESP allocation and ratable recognition, complicating the royalty-exception model.
- Contract modification risk is elevated: any feature scope change or rate change reopens the 606 analysis.

**Pricing Guardrails**
- `gross_margin_absolute_floor` → **BLOCK** (0% actual vs. 25% threshold) — structural artifact of $0 price, but must be resolved via CFO exception or alternative structure.
- `gross_margin_warn` → WARN (0% vs. 40% target).

sections.heading: Approval Routing
sections.content_markdown:
| Step | Approver | Veto? | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|-------|
| 1 | AE Manager — Sarah Goldstein | No | Intake confirmation |
| 2 (parallel) | **CFO** | ✅ Yes | Zero-margin guardrail exception; 606 revenue model sign-off |
| 2 (parallel) | **Legal** | ✅ Yes | White-label scope, attribution, minimum guarantee, audit rights, early-access NDA |
| 2 (parallel) | **RevOps** | No | Partnership governance, reporting cadence, audit controls |
| 3 | **CEO** | ✅ Yes | Final approval; partnership deals require CEO per policy |

**Estimated cycle time: 10–12 business days** (gated by parallel CFO + Legal alignment before CEO can engage)

**Must clear before Legal sends counters:**
1. CFO selects alternative pricing structure (or grants explicit 0-margin override)
2. Legal scopes white-label grant (non-transferable, attributed, surface-limited)
3. CFO + Legal jointly confirm ASC 606 principal-vs-agent classification
4. RevOps establishes quarterly reconciliation and annual true-up framework

sections.heading: Recommendation
sections.content_markdown: **Ship with conditions** — the strategic opportunity is real (distribution leverage into 8 top-50 Clay customers, $800K midpoint annual rev-share, rebuild risk converted to partnership), but the deal cannot proceed as proposed: the white-label clause is an unscoped IP license, the rev-share has no floor or audit rights, and the 0% margin structure triggers a hard CFO block — all three must be resolved before counters go out.

comms.reasoning_summary: Calibrated customer email to collaborative tone given the deal context. AE email leads with verdict (ship-with-conditions (blockers must clear first)) and a numbered next-step list. One-pager and Slack post both headline the recommendation and surface 4 flagged redlines (4 countered in customer reply). Approval routing summary: ~12 business days through 5-step chain.

synthesis: Pricing fires a block_absolute guardrail on 0% margin because list and proposed prices are both $0, so the rev-share economics need explicit CFO override against actual white-label COGS before this can move. ASC 606 treatment leans on the sales/usage royalty exception, but six red flags — distinct co-marketing and early-access POs, principal-vs-agent, and consideration-payable-to-a-customer — keep contract modification risk live and require auditor review. Redline is a hard block: the white-label grant lacks scope limits, non-transferability, and attribution, and the rev-share has no minimum guarantee or audit rights against an estimated $400K–$1.2M annual range. Approval requires parallel CFO, Legal, and RevOps clearance before CEO sign-off, a ~12 business day cycle with low confidence given no precedent. Recommendation: hold and re-scope — do not ship until white-label scope, rev-share floor with audit rights, and CFO margin override are resolved, then proceed with the collaborative customer tone already drafted.

signals.headline: Reverberate Growth crosses 50 active mid-market clients, formalizes 'Reverberate Engine' practice
signals.summary: GTM agency Reverberate Growth announced its 50th active client and the formal launch of 'Reverberate Engine,' a productized service line that bundles outbound and enrichment workflows for mid-market RevOps teams.

signals.headline: Reverberate founder publishes 2026 GTM Operator Playbook, 14k subscribers in two weeks
signals.summary: Reverberate Growth's founder released the '2026 GTM Operator Playbook,' a long-form essay on agency-led RevOps that gained 14,000 subscribers in its first two weeks. The post argues for vendor-agnostic agency partnerships over single-vendor lock-in.

signals.headline: Reverberate Growth named #18 on annual 'Top 50 GTM Agencies' list
signals.summary: Reverberate Growth ranked #18 on the annual 'Top 50 GTM Agencies' list (up from #34 the prior year), with judges citing strong client outcomes in enrichment-heavy outbound and waterfall enrichment design.

signals.headline: GTM agencies announce vendor partnerships at 2026 RevOps Summit
signals.summary: At the RevOps Summit, several top-50 GTM agencies — including Reverberate — were named in vendor co-launch sessions. Industry observers describe a shift toward agency-vendor revenue-share partnerships rather than referral fees.

signals.headline: Reverberate hires former PLG-vendor head of partnerships
signals.summary: Reverberate Growth hired a former head of partnerships from a major PLG vendor to lead its new 'Strategic Vendor Partnerships' practice — explicitly framed as a path to revenue-share deals with infrastructure providers.
